poem:
**Kids' Quiz**

Sir John Harington plumbed a throne for a queen,  
a small white sentence ending in a flush.  
The *Victoria*'s crew stitched the world shut—  
needle and wake—came home with empty pockets  
and a rounder map.  

Meanwhile a mosasaurus unhinges,  
bones loose as gossip,  
and somewhere a dog practises its face:  
however many muscles it takes to look loved.
prompt: You are the poet Simon Armitage. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Who invented toilets and who was first to go around the world? The kids’ quiz <li class="quiz__question question" data-text="Emi, 7, asks: who invented toilets? ">Emi, 7, asks: who invented toilets? <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="Archimedes " data-correct="false">Archimedes <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="Sir John Harington " data-correct="true">Sir John Harington <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="Beatrix Potter " data-correct="false">Beatrix Potter <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="Prince Albert " data-correct="false">Prince Albert <li class="quiz__question question" data-text="Rosie, 6, asks: who was the first person to travel around the world? ">Rosie, 6, asks: who was the first person to travel around the world? <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="Amelia Earhart " data-correct="false">Amelia Earhart <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="Sir Francis Drake " data-correct="false">Sir Francis Drake <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="Genghis Khan " data-correct="false">Genghis Khan <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="The crew of the ship Victoria " data-correct="true">The crew of the ship Victoria <li class="quiz__question question" data-text="Billy, 5, asks: how could a mosasaurus eat so much food in one go? ">Billy, 5, asks: how could a mosasaurus eat so much food in one go? <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="They invented and used knives and forks " data-correct="false">They invented and used knives and forks <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="They had three separate mouths " data-correct="false">They had three separate mouths <li class="question__answer answer" data-text="They had loosely joined bones that could flex " data-correct="true">They had loosely joined bones that could flex They liquidised their food in their mouths <li class="quiz__question question" data-text="Finn, 11, asks: how many muscles are there in a dog’s face? ">Finn, 11, asks: how many muscles are there in a dog’s face?". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
